channel.title: Comments for page "Light-Dependent Reactions"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Light-Dependent Reactions"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the bit at the end where she cheats on her perfume. It's a good way to channel her… condition… in a harmless way and contain the knowledge of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am eagerly awaiting a tale that introduces Cactusman to Alpha-Niner so that he and Doctor Elliott can team up and save the day via spiked justice and the power of autotrophism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I expect it will be much more tolerable when the full series has been laid out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because many of the tales are essentially the following
- [Author avatar] is introduced
- [Author avatar] meets with an O5/Foundation higher-up
- [Author avatar] is told about Alpha-9
- [Author avatar] expresses incredulity that anyone would want to repeat the shitshow that was Omega-7
- O5/Foundation higher-up convinces/coerces [author avatar] into joining Alpha-9

with some variation. The only significant deviation from this that I've seen have been the first two tales. I don't mind reading stories that are similar in structure a few times, provided that they're well-executed. But this is edging closer and closer to double-digit territory, and there's relatively little to show for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really like these kind of tales. While they aren't suitable for the entire series, they are good for building up to the main events of the series so we know what the hell is going on before the real action begins, which is something I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I'm confused by this reaction. This is clearly a long form work, and still has had substantially less build up than even a short novel might.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very well-done build-up is still build-up, and I'm pretty sick of the continuous cavalcade of "Meet the Team" tales that, at this point, make up around 80% of the works in this series.

By itself, this work would more than merit an upvote. However, because it's part of larger work, it gets judged in the context of the other parts of that work. So no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great! I enjoyed it very much. I liked the pacing with the interview and narratives, everything really. You obviously know what you're doing.
+1 is a given.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why I didn't put two and two together after reading 2108 (probably because I didn't check to see who wrote that one). Anyways, nice work. I like this character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, she's the same person. :D Chelsea is never going drinking with Adams again. Ever. She'll stay home and synthesize ridiculously fancy tea blends, thankyouverymuch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good story.
I imagined that first flashback scene in movie/montage/game intro style. It was kinda nice. :)

But wait a sec…
Elliotts name is Chelsea, right? And she has close friend named Blarie, right?
Plus, She had been assigned to A-9.
Just like a girl on certian night out.
Coincidence?

EDIT. Damn, almost forgotten: +1'd ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So many potential botany puns, so little time.

+1'd with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd actually be very interested in seeing other Staff avatars with powers introduced into the Resurrection, provided their powers are as well-written as this.

Perhaps I'll throw my hat into the ring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<_< You saw nothing.

Good catch, though. Thanks. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your condition is classified 5/2081/E-46693.

I might be wrong, but considering the SCP involved, was 2081 supposed to be 2108?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Point taken. Let me see what I can do about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, kind of an interesting circumstance here. My real complaint is that, final scene aside, it's just an extension/elaboration on the event log in that SCP article. It didn't feel terribly novel, but I'm not downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read a draft of this in chat, and it was good, but the final version is incredibly good. I really enjoyed this, it's probably one of my favorite Resurrection story so far. Good work, Photosynthetic. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, she's definitely going to be turning up in Alpha-Nine tales, and not just in my own work. :D But yes, I'm currently mulling over a few tale ideas starring her. It'll be fun!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I was not prepared for that ending… Great job, Photo! Do you plan on leaving this alone, or perhaps writing some tales about Elliot's adventures with MTF Alpha-Nine? I'm curious to know how she would work into a MTF, to be honest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another member, huh? Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very Nice, Photo!

+1'd

item.title: Author's Notes
item.content:encoded:
Another Resurrection tale: a new conscript for Alpha-Nine.

Thanks for thedeadlymoose, Clef, Decibelles, Gaffsey, Palaios, Piwahnah, Tuomey Tombstone, Blaroth, and many more people for feedback in chat!
